claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

Please let me know which applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please tell me each of their ages (and their names if you'd like, to help keep track).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Oh hello, yes please! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible.

I have three children actually - Alex who's 3, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 2. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely to meet you! Thank you for providing those details. So you have three children:

- **Alex** - 3 years old
- **Blake** - 8 years old
- **Charlie** - 2 years old

Since all three children are under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. That's a great start!

Now let me check the next requirement for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
The next question is about whether any of your children are working.

**Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

Given their ages (3, 8, and 2), I would assume none of them are in paid employment - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, none of them are working at all - they're just little ones!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense - they're all still very young. So they definitely meet this requirement too.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Are all three children NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This would include benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed in their own name. Given their young ages (3, 8, and 2), I'd expect this wouldn't apply to them - is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - none of them are receiving any benefits in their own names.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's another requirement met for all three children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now checking about apprenticeships:

**Are any of your children currently in an apprenticeship?**

Again, given their ages (3, 8, and 2), I would expect they're not in any apprenticeships - is that right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in apprenticeships - they're far too young for that!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly right! They're all much too young for apprenticeships. That's another box ticked for all three.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** AND you're working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ months)
4. You have **pre-settled status** AND you're actively looking for work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. You have **pre-settled status** AND you have sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain income-based benefits)
6. You have **pre-settled status** AND you're a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK - I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful! Living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about your responsibility for the children.

**Are you responsible for Alex, Blake, and Charlie?**

You qualify as responsible if **any one** of these applies:

1. **You live with the children**
2. **You contribute to their upkeep** (e.g., paying for food, clothes, pocket money, housing, or presents - at least the equivalent value of Child Benefit)
3. **You pay maintenance** for them under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they all live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's great - living with the children means you're clearly responsible for them. Let's move on.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good progress! Now an important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?**

For example:
- Another parent
- A grandparent
- Another family member or guardian

Please let me know if:
- **No one else is claiming** for any of them
- **Someone else is claiming**, but you've agreed that you should be the one to claim instead
- **There's a dispute** about who should claim

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that keeps things straightforward. Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about the children's living situation.

**Where are Alex, Blake, and Charlie currently living?**

Are any of them:
- **In hospital**
- **In local authority care**
- **Being fostered**

Or are they all:
- **Living normally at home with you**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're all living normally at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Nearly there now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
This is the final question:

**Is it the case that your local council is NOT paying towards any of the children's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, the council isn't contributing financially towards housing or looking after Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - the council isn't paying anything towards them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Wonderful news! That completes the assessment, and I'm pleased to tell you the outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

Based on your answers, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for all three of your children:**

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| **Alex** | 3 | ✅ Yes |
| **Blake** | 8 | ✅ Yes |
| **Charlie** | 2 | ✅ Yes |

### Why You Qualify:
- ✅ All three children are under 16
- ✅ None are working, receiving benefits, or in apprenticeships
- ✅ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✅ You live with all three children
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them
- ✅ The local council isn't paying towards their care

---

### 📝 Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online** at [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim) or by post using **form CH2**
2. **You'll need to provide:**
   - Proof of your identity
   - Birth certificates for Alex, Blake, and Charlie

3. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receive certain benefits)

---

### ⚠️ Important Note:
If you or your partner earns over **£60,000** per year, you may be affected by the **High Income Child Benefit Charge**. You can still claim, but some or all of the benefit may need to be repaid through tax. You can choose to receive the payments or just register your claim to protect your National Insurance record.

---

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 3) qualifies because: (1) Under 16 years old - meets age requirement automatically, (2) Not in paid work, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, (6) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, and (8) Local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) qualifies because: (1) Under 16 years old - meets age requirement automatically, (2) Not in paid work, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, (6) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, and (8) Local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) qualifies because: (1) Under 16 years old - meets age requirement automatically, (2) Not in paid work, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, (6) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, and (8) Local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16 and meet the qualifying child criteria. The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with all three children (demonstrating responsibility), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 3) qualifies because: (1) Under 16 years old - meets age requirement automatically, (2) Not in paid work, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, (6) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, and (8) Local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) qualifies because: (1) Under 16 years old - meets age requirement automatically, (2) Not in paid work, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, (6) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, and (8) Local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Charlie (age 2) qualifies because: (1) Under 16 years old - meets age requirement automatically, (2) Not in paid work, (3) Not receiving benefits in their own right, (4) Not in an apprenticeship, (5) Lives with the claimant who normally resides in the UK, (6) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, and (8) Local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie). All children are under 16 and meet the qualifying child criteria. The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), lives with all three children (demonstrating responsibility), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
